{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resources (HR) Officer",
  "description": "Maroriem Consults is a Nigeria-based accounting and consulting firm providing bookkeeping, tax compliance, payroll, and financial advisory services to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) operating within Nigeria. We support our clients with accurate financial reporting and regulatory compliance while maintaining strong internal financial governance as a professional services firm.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources (HR) Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos (Remote)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Overview


	To support the design, implementation, and delivery of HR services for both Maroriem internal operations and client organizations, ensuring compliance, efficiency, and alignment with business objectives.


Key Responsibilities
Recruitment &amp; Talent Acquisition:


	Support end-to-end recruitment for internal roles and client organizations
	Draft job descriptions and job advertisements
	Screen candidates, coordinate interviews, and manage candidate communication
	Maintain talent pipelines and recruitment databases


HR Operations &amp; Administration:


	Maintain accurate employee records and HR databases (internal and client accounts)
	Prepare employment contracts, onboarding, and exit documentation
	Manage onboarding and offboarding processes
	Ensure proper documentation and filing (physical and digital)


Client HR Support (Consulting Function):


	Provide HR advisory support to client organizations
	Assist in developing HR policies, employee handbooks, and frameworks
	Support client recruitment, performance management, and disciplinary processes
	Ensure client compliance with applicable labor laws and HR best practices


Payroll &amp; Benefits Administration:


	Support payroll processing for clients and internal staff
	Validate payroll inputs (attendance, leave, deductions, etc.)
	Ensure accuracy and timeliness of payroll reports
	Administer employee benefits (e.g., HMO, pensions)


Performance Management:


	Support the implementation of performance management systems
	Track employee KPIs and appraisal cycles
	Assist in developing and monitoring Employee Performance Improvement Plans (EPIPs)


Learning &amp; Development:


	Identify training needs (internal and client organizations)
	Coordinate training sessions and track participation
	Maintain training records and evaluate training effectiveness


Employee Relations &amp; Compliance:


	Handle employee inquiries and resolve HR-related issues
	Support disciplinary processes and investigations
	Ensure compliance with labor laws and company policies
	Promote a positive work culture and employee engagement


HR Reporting &amp; Analytics:


	Prepare HR reports (e.g., headcount, turnover, recruitment metrics)
	Maintain HR dashboards for internal and client reporting
	Provide data-driven insights to support decision-making


Qualification &amp; Requirements
Educational Qualification and Experience:


	
	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Psychology, or a related field with&nbsp;1&ndash;2 years of relevant HR experience
	


Knowledge &amp; Skills:


	Basic knowledge of HR practices and labor laws
	Understanding of payroll processes and basic accounting principles
	Strong administrative and organizational skills
	Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
	High level of attention to detail and confidentiality
	Ability to manage multiple clients and projects simultaneously


Added Advantage:


	Student or Associate Membership of:
	
		Chartered Institute of Personnel Management of Nigeria
		Society for Human Resource Management
		Human Resource Certification Institute
	
	
",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-19",
  "validThrough": "2026-03-27",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

Human Resources (HR) Officer Job at Maroriem Consults




        
    

        
        Maroriem Consults is a Nigeria-based accounting and consulting firm providing bookkeeping, tax compliance, payroll, and financial advisory services to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) operating within Nigeria. We support our clients with accurate financial reporting and regulatory compliance while maintaining strong internal financial governance as a professional services firm.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources (HR) Officer

Job Location: Lagos (Remote)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	To support the design, implementation, and delivery of HR services for both Maroriem internal operations and client organizations, ensuring compliance, efficiency, and alignment with business objectives.


Key Responsibilities
Recruitment & Talent Acquisition:


	Support end-to-end recruitment for internal roles and client organizations
	Draft job descriptions and job advertisements
	Screen candidates, coordinate interviews, and manage candidate communication
	Maintain talent pipelines and recruitment databases


HR Operations & Administration:


	Maintain accurate employee records and HR databases (internal and client accounts)
	Prepare employment contracts, onboarding, and exit documentation
	Manage onboarding and offboarding processes
	Ensure proper documentation and filing (physical and digital)


Client HR Support (Consulting Function):


	Provide HR advisory support to client organizations
	Assist in developing HR policies, employee handbooks, and frameworks
	Support client recruitment, performance management, and disciplinary processes
	Ensure client compliance with applicable labor laws and HR best practices


Payroll & Benefits Administration:


	Support payroll processing for clients and internal staff
	Validate payroll inputs (attendance, leave, deductions, etc.)
	Ensure accuracy and timeliness of payroll reports
	Administer employee benefits (e.g., HMO, pensions)


Performance Management:


	Support the implementation of performance management systems
	Track employee KPIs and appraisal cycles
	Assist in developing and monitoring Employee Performance Improvement Plans (EPIPs)


Learning & Development:


	Identify training needs (internal and client organizations)
	Coordinate training sessions and track participation
	Maintain training records and evaluate training effectiveness


Employee Relations & Compliance:


	Handle employee inquiries and resolve HR-related issues
	Support disciplinary processes and investigations
	Ensure compliance with labor laws and company policies
	Promote a positive work culture and employee engagement


HR Reporting & Analytics:


	Prepare HR reports (e.g., headcount, turnover, recruitment metrics)
	Maintain HR dashboards for internal and client reporting
	Provide data-driven insights to support decision-making


Qualification & Requirements
Educational Qualification and Experience:


	
	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Psychology, or a related field with 1–2 years of relevant HR experience
	


Knowledge & Skills:


	Basic knowledge of HR practices and labor laws
	Understanding of payroll processes and basic accounting principles
	Strong administrative and organizational skills
	Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
	High level of attention to detail and confidentiality
	Ability to manage multiple clients and projects simultaneously


Added Advantage:


	Student or Associate Membership of:
	
		Chartered Institute of Personnel Management of Nigeria
		Society for Human Resource Management
		Human Resource Certification Institute
	
	
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Excellence Manager",
  "description": "Zipline - Do you want to change the world? Zipline uses drones to deliver critical and life-saving medicine to thousands of hospitals serving millions of people in multiple countries. Our mission is to provide every human on Earth with instant access to vital medical supplies. Join Zipline and help us make this a reality for billions of people.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Excellence Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja, Nigeria

About You and The Role


	The Operations Excellence Manager plays a critical role in enhancing the efficiency, quality, and safety of Zipline&rsquo;s operations.
	In this role, you will build and scale the quality, safety, and operational governance systems that enable country teams to run high-performing, compliant operations with strong local ownership.
	You&rsquo;ll evolve the tools, standards, documentation, and training that drive consistent performance across operational sites, strengthen audit and incident management processes, and lead continuous improvement initiatives across the network.
	This role requires strong systems thinking and a scalability mindset. Reporting to the Country Head of Product, you&rsquo;ll work closely with Nest Leads, Compliance Officers, Zipline Academy, and Engineering and Regulatory teams to raise operational standards while empowering country teams to execute locally.


What You&rsquo;ll Do
Quality &amp; Safety Governance Systems:


	Design, maintain, and evolve Zipline&rsquo;s global quality, safety, and compliance systems, including playbooks, manuals, and tools, to ensure consistent, compliant, and high-performing operations.
	Partner with internal stakeholders to establish and maintain governance structures, documentation standards, and oversight tools that enable nests to own execution.
	Define and track country-level quality and safety objectives, KPIs, and performance metrics in collaboration with leadership, driving accountability and data-driven improvement.
	Develop and oversee audit frameworks and tools that enable nests to self-assess compliance, while leading or supporting regulatory, safety, and customer audits, incident investigations, and root cause analyses.
	Collaborate closely with nest compliance teams to ensure consistent execution of global and regional standards.


Continuous Improvement, Scalability &amp; Documentation:


	Partner with Country Heads of Product and Nest Leads to refine SOPs, technical documentation, and workflows for greater efficiency and scalability.
	Work with the Excellence &amp; Insights team to design metrics, dashboards, and oversight tools that enable Zipline to monitor and improve quality at scale.
	Identify opportunities to automate, standardize, or streamline processes so that quality improves as the business grows, without requiring more manual oversight.


Training &amp; Capability Enablement:


	Partner with Zipline Academy to develop and deliver scalable training programs on safety, and quality compliance best practices that ensures ownership at the nest level.
	Drive adoption of new standards, processes, and tools through effective change management and coaching.
	Ensure knowledge transfer, documentation, and tools are designed for replicability across new sites and markets.


What You&rsquo;ll Bring


	4+ years of experience in operations excellence, quality, or safety systems within a regulated industry (aviation, logistics, healthcare, or manufacturing).&nbsp;
	Experience leading continuous improvement or operational excellence programs.
	Proven ability to design systems, tools, or processes that improve quality and safety.
	Strong project management, communication, and stakeholder engagement skills.
	Highly proactive in identifying potential risks or gaps before they escalate, and confident in flagging them to leadership with actionable solutions.
	Mission-driven, adaptable, and energized by solving complex operational challenges.
	Certifications in Lean Six Sigma, Operational Excellence, or ISO standards are a plus.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-20",

Operations Excellence Manager Job at Zipline




        
    

        
        Zipline - Do you want to change the world? Zipline uses drones to deliver critical and life-saving medicine to thousands of hospitals serving millions of people in multiple countries. Our mission is to provide every human on Earth with instant access to vital medical supplies. Join Zipline and help us make this a reality for billions of people.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Excellence Manager

Job Location: Abuja, Nigeria

About You and The Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Operations Excellence Manager plays a critical role in enhancing the efficiency, quality, and safety of Zipline’s operations.
	In this role, you will build and scale the quality, safety, and operational governance systems that enable country teams to run high-performing, compliant operations with strong local ownership.
	You’ll evolve the tools, standards, documentation, and training that drive consistent performance across operational sites, strengthen audit and incident management processes, and lead continuous improvement initiatives across the network.
	This role requires strong systems thinking and a scalability mindset. Reporting to the Country Head of Product, you’ll work closely with Nest Leads, Compliance Officers, Zipline Academy, and Engineering and Regulatory teams to raise operational standards while empowering country teams to execute locally.


What You’ll Do
Quality & Safety Governance Systems:


	Design, maintain, and evolve Zipline’s global quality, safety, and compliance systems, including playbooks, manuals, and tools, to ensure consistent, compliant, and high-performing operations.
	Partner with internal stakeholders to establish and maintain governance structures, documentation standards, and oversight tools that enable nests to own execution.
	Define and track country-level quality and safety objectives, KPIs, and performance metrics in collaboration with leadership, driving accountability and data-driven improvement.
	Develop and oversee audit frameworks and tools that enable nests to self-assess compliance, while leading or supporting regulatory, safety, and customer audits, incident investigations, and root cause analyses.
	Collaborate closely with nest compliance teams to ensure consistent execution of global and regional standards.


Continuous Improvement, Scalability & Documentation:


	Partner with Country Heads of Product and Nest Leads to refine SOPs, technical documentation, and workflows for greater efficiency and scalability.
	Work with the Excellence & Insights team to design metrics, dashboards, and oversight tools that enable Zipline to monitor and improve quality at scale.
	Identify opportunities to automate, standardize, or streamline processes so that quality improves as the business grows, without requiring more manual oversight.


Training & Capability Enablement:


	Partner with Zipline Academy to develop and deliver scalable training programs on safety, and quality compliance best practices that ensures ownership at the nest level.
	Drive adoption of new standards, processes, and tools through effective change management and coaching.
	Ensure knowledge transfer, documentation, and tools are designed for replicability across new sites and markets.


What You’ll Bring


	4+ years of experience in operations excellence, quality, or safety systems within a regulated industry (aviation, logistics, healthcare, or manufacturing). 
	Experience leading continuous improvement or operational excellence programs.
	Proven ability to design systems, tools, or processes that improve quality and safety.
	Strong project management, communication, and stakeholder engagement skills.
	Highly proactive in identifying potential risks or gaps before they escalate, and confident in flagging them to leadership with actionable solutions.
	Mission-driven, adaptable, and energized by solving complex operational challenges.
	Certifications in Lean Six Sigma, Operational Excellence, or ISO standards are a plus.

	Promote product through online and offline channels
	Conduct market research to identify trends and customer needs etc.

Sales and Marketing Officer Job at Soteria Eye Clinic




        
    

        
        Soteria Eye Clinic is a private enterprise registered in Nigeria as a limited liability company to offer high quality, affordable and accessible comprehensive world class eye care services to our immediate community and environs. Our purpose is to create and deliver first class comprehensive eye care services to ALL in order to “Improve Lives through Affordable Quality Eye Care.”

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales and Marketing Officer

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilties
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop and implement sales and marketing strategies
	Promote product through online and offline channels
	Conduct market research to identify trends and customer needs etc.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess an OND / NCE / HND qualification with 1 - 2 years of experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Electrical Technician",
  "description": "Golden Oil Industries Limited was incorporated in Nigeria on 8th September, 1988, and has grown into a household name in Nigeria&#39;s edible oil&#39;s industry. The company&#39;s focus is to provide healthy oil as a cooking medium to its customers. The company is Nigeria&#39;s trusted name for supply of refined palm oil, palm kernel oil, olein, soybean Oil, stearin, soybean meal, soy lecithin, and palm kernel de-oiled cake. Our oils are used as healthy cooking medium. Soybean meal and palm kernel de-oiled cake are used in premier poultry / animal feed.

The company&#39;s products have been duly certified by the appropriate regulatory agencies Such as the National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC), Standard Organization of Nigeria (SON) and National Environmental Standards and Regulations Enforcement Agency (NESREA). Golden Oil has modern processing technology and plants, which can be compared to worldwide standards. The company has highly trained seasoned oil experts, who&#39;s commitment is to delivering healthy oil to Nigerian consumers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Technician

Job Location:&nbsp;Anambra
Job type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Electrical Technician will be responsible for installing, maintaining, and repairing electrical systems in our facility.
	The ideal candidate should have a strong understanding of electrical safety protocols, excellent problem-solving skills, and a keen attention to detail, while upholding the highest standards of housekeeping and adherence to Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) guidelines.


Responsibilities


	Electrical Routine Activities: Install, maintain, and repair electrical control, wiring, and lighting systems, and repair electrical wiring and equipment. Troubleshoot electrical issues and implement solutions to ensure an uninterrupted power supply to machinery and equipment.
	Maintenance and Repairs:&nbsp;inspect electrical components, identify electrical problems, and troubleshoot issues using appropriate testing devices, including motors, transformers, switches, and circuit breakers.
	Emergency Response: Respond promptly to electrical and instrumentation emergencies, diagnose issues, and implement solutions to minimize downtime and maintain operational continuity.
	Troubleshooting: Analyze electrical issues and implement solutions promptly, minimizing downtime and ensuring continuous operations.
	Safety Compliance: Adhere to electrical safety protocols and regulations. Conduct safety assessments and implement measures to prevent electrical hazards.
	Housekeeping and GMP: Ensure compliance with safety and quality standards, including housekeeping and Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) in the work area.
	Documentation: Keep detailed records of maintenance activities, repairs, calibration procedures, and inventory. Prepare accurate reports and documentation as required.
	Collaboration: Collaborate with engineers, electricians, and other professionals to identify and implement solutions for electrical problems. Work closely with other departments and external vendors for specialized repairs and equipment upgrades. Provide technical support and training to other staff members as needed.


The Person


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or Diploma in Electrical Electronics, Electrical Technology, or a related field.
	Must have 3-5 years of experience operating refining equipment in the food processing industry, specifically in vegetable oil processing.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in electrical technician maintenance, preferably in a manufacturing or GMP-regulated environment.
	Knowledge of industrial electrical systems, including motors, motor controls, and programmable logic controllers (PLCs).
	Physical ability to perform tasks that may require prolonged standing, lifting, and working in confined spaces.
	Ability to conduct precise quality checks, maintain accurate records, and ensure cleanliness, meeting GMP cleanliness standards and SOP.
	Good communication skills to coordinate with team members and report operational status effectively.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;nnenna@goldenoiltd.com&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;bassey@activa.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-19",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Golden Oil Industries Limited"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",

Electrical Technician Job at Golden Oil Industries Limited




        
    

        
        
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    

Golden Oil Industries Limited was incorporated in Nigeria on 8th September, 1988, and has grown into a household name in Nigeria's edible oil's industry. The company's focus is to provide healthy oil as a cooking medium to its customers. The company is Nigeria's trusted name for supply of refined palm oil, palm kernel oil, olein, soybean Oil, stearin, soybean meal, soy lecithin, and palm kernel de-oiled cake. Our oils are used as healthy cooking medium. Soybean meal and palm kernel de-oiled cake are used in premier poultry / animal feed.

The company's products have been duly certified by the appropriate regulatory agencies Such as the National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC), Standard Organization of Nigeria (SON) and National Environmental Standards and Regulations Enforcement Agency (NESREA). Golden Oil has modern processing technology and plants, which can be compared to worldwide standards. The company has highly trained seasoned oil experts, who's commitment is to delivering healthy oil to Nigerian consumers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Technician

Job Location: Anambra
Job type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Electrical Technician will be responsible for installing, maintaining, and repairing electrical systems in our facility.
	The ideal candidate should have a strong understanding of electrical safety protocols, excellent problem-solving skills, and a keen attention to detail, while upholding the highest standards of housekeeping and adherence to Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) guidelines.


Responsibilities


	Electrical Routine Activities: Install, maintain, and repair electrical control, wiring, and lighting systems, and repair electrical wiring and equipment. Troubleshoot electrical issues and implement solutions to ensure an uninterrupted power supply to machinery and equipment.
	Maintenance and Repairs: inspect electrical components, identify electrical problems, and troubleshoot issues using appropriate testing devices, including motors, transformers, switches, and circuit breakers.
	Emergency Response: Respond promptly to electrical and instrumentation emergencies, diagnose issues, and implement solutions to minimize downtime and maintain operational continuity.
	Troubleshooting: Analyze electrical issues and implement solutions promptly, minimizing downtime and ensuring continuous operations.
	Safety Compliance: Adhere to electrical safety protocols and regulations. Conduct safety assessments and implement measures to prevent electrical hazards.
	Housekeeping and GMP: Ensure compliance with safety and quality standards, including housekeeping and Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) in the work area.
	Documentation: Keep detailed records of maintenance activities, repairs, calibration procedures, and inventory. Prepare accurate reports and documentation as required.
	Collaboration: Collaborate with engineers, electricians, and other professionals to identify and implement solutions for electrical problems. Work closely with other departments and external vendors for specialized repairs and equipment upgrades. Provide technical support and training to other staff members as needed.


The Person


	Bachelor's Degree or Diploma in Electrical Electronics, Electrical Technology, or a related field.
	Must have 3-5 years of experience operating refining equipment in the food processing industry, specifically in vegetable oil processing.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in electrical technician maintenance, preferably in a manufacturing or GMP-regulated environment.
	Knowledge of industrial electrical systems, including motors, motor controls, and programmable logic controllers (PLCs).
	Physical ability to perform tasks that may require prolonged standing, lifting, and working in confined spaces.
	Ability to conduct precise quality checks, maintain accurate records, and ensure cleanliness, meeting GMP cleanliness standards and SOP.
	Good communication skills to coordinate with team members and report operational status effectively.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] & [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the    

    

        Click here to Apply

Human Resource Business Partner Job at Castel Resources Consultancy Limited




        
    

        
        Castel Resources Consultancy Limited unlocks your business's full potential with our comprehensive HR outsourcing services. Our expert team handles everything from recruitment and payroll processing to employee benefits administration and compliance, freeing you from administrative burdens and ensuring legal compliance. With advanced HR technology, strategic planning, and dedicated support for employee development and relations, we streamline your HR functions, allowing you to focus on your core business activities and drive growth. Partner with us to elevate your HR capabilities and achieve seamless, efficient operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Business Partner
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Responsible for managing HR operations and aligning HR strategies with business objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee recruitment and onboarding
	Manage employee relations and performance
	Implement HR policies and procedures
	Handle training and development initiatives
	Ensure compliance with labor laws.


Requirements


	Degree in HR, Business Administration, or related fields
	4–7 years of experience
	Strong interpersonal and problem-solving skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
